title: Temporary Residence
description: Learn the documents, rules and Slovak phrases you need for your temporary residence permit

exercises.title: Key Documents
exercises.subtitle: These are the documents you will need. Learn their Slovak names — the officer will use them.

scenarios.situation: The officer at the Foreign Police says: 'Prosím váš cestovný pas.' What are they asking for?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Cestovný pas = passport. This is always the first thing they ask for.

scenarios.situation: The officer says: 'Výpis z registra trestov nesmie byť starší ako 90 dní.' What is the rule?
choices: The criminal record must be at least 90 days old
The criminal record cannot be older than 90 days
You need 90 days to get the criminal record
The criminal record is valid for 90 years
scenarios.answer: The criminal record cannot be older than 90 days
scenarios.answerMeaning: Nesmie byť starší ako = must not be older than. This is a critical rule — many applications are rejected because of an expired criminal record.

scenarios.situation: Your criminal record from home was issued 4 months ago. Can you use it for your application?
choices: Yes — if it has an apostille
No — it must be less than 90 days old
scenarios.answer: No — it must be less than 90 days old
scenarios.answerMeaning: 4 months = approximately 120 days. The limit is 90 days. You need to order a new one.

scenarios.situation: You have a temporary residence for employment but you want to start a business on the side. Is this allowed?
choices: Yes — you can do both with one permit
No — the permit is for one purpose only
Only if the business makes less than €500/month
scenarios.answer: No — the permit is for one purpose only
scenarios.answerMeaning: A temporary residence permit is bound to ONE purpose. Employment â‰  business. You would need a separate permit for business activity.

scenarios.situation: The officer asks: 'Doklad o ubytovaní, prosím.' Your landlord has not given you anything yet. What should you do?
scenarios.instruction: Choose the best course of action
choices: Show your rental contract — it is enough
Ask your landlord for a signed and notarised statement
scenarios.answer: Ask your landlord for a signed and notarised statement
scenarios.answerMeaning: The landlord must provide a notarised confirmation (úradne overené vyhlásenie) OR your signed lease. A hotel booking is not accepted for longer stays.

exercises.title: At the Foreign Police — Key Phrases
exercises.subtitle: What you will say and hear at the office.

rows.label: Opening
rows.slovak: Chcel/a by som požiadať o prechodný pobyt.
rows.english: I would like to apply for a temporary residence permit.

rows.label: Appointment
rows.english: I have an appointment at ten o'clock.

rows.label: Timeline
rows.english: When will the decision be ready?

rows.label: Not clear
rows.english: I don't understand. Can you write it down?

words.slovak: Chcel by som požiadať o prechodný pobyt.
words.english: I would like to apply for a temporary residence permit.
When will the decision be ready?

items.sentence: Chcel by som požiadať o ___ pobyt.
items.translation: I would like to apply for a ___ residence permit.

items.sentence: Výpis z registra trestov nesmie byť starší ako ___ dní.
items.translation: The criminal record must not be older than ___ days.
I have an ___ at ten o'clock.
The administrative fee is ___ euros fifty.

items.sentence: Dokument musí mať úradný ___ do slovenčiny.
items.translation: The document must have a certified ___ into Slovak.

scenarios.situation: The officer says your application is not complete: 'Žiadosť nie je kompletná.' What do you ask?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Asking which documents are missing is the most useful — you need to know exactly what to bring next time.

scenarios.situation: How much is the administrative fee for a temporary residence permit?
scenarios.answerMeaning: The current fee is €165.50. Always check minv.sk before your appointment as fees can change.

scenarios.situation: You submitted your application 60 days ago and have not heard back. What should you know?
choices: Processing takes up to 90 days — this is normal
scenarios.answer: Processing takes up to 90 days — this is normal
scenarios.answerMeaning: The legal processing time is up to 90 days. You are authorised to stay in Slovakia while waiting.

scenarios.situation: Your current temporary residence expires in 2 weeks. When should you have applied for renewal?
choices: On the day it expires — that is the deadline
Within 30 days after expiry — there is a grace period
scenarios.answerMeaning: Slovak law requires you to apply before your permit expires — there is no fixed minimum number of days, but the Foreign Police recommend applying well in advance since processing takes up to 90 days. If you apply before expiry you can legally stay while waiting for the decision.

scenarios.situation: Your documents from home country need an apostille. Where do you get an apostille?
choices: At the Slovak embassy in your home country
From the authority that issued the document in your home country
scenarios.answer: From the authority that issued the document in your home country
scenarios.answerMeaning: The apostille is attached by the relevant authority in the country that issued the document — for example, the Ministry of Justice or designated court. Not from Slovakia.

exercises.context: You need a document from your landlord for the Foreign Police. You are texting him now.

A.title: Document arranged! ✓
A.description: Pán Novák will have the notarised letter ready by Friday.

B.title: You explained it well! ✓
B.description: You corrected the landlord and got the right document sorted.

nodes.speaker: user
nodes.prompt: Start the conversation. How do you greet your landlord?

nodes.speaker: landlord
nodes.wrongAnswerTranslation: Hmm... what are you talking about?

choices.translation: I need one document from you.
I have a question about the flat.

nodes.speaker: landlord
nodes.wrongAnswerResponse: To nie je dokument... čo presne potrebujete?
nodes.wrongAnswerTranslation: That's not a document... what exactly do you need?

choices.translation: A statement that I live at your place.

nodes.speaker: landlord
nodes.translation: What do you need it for?
nodes.wrongAnswerTranslation: I don't understand... can you repeat that?

choices.translation: I am applying for temporary residence.

nodes.speaker: landlord
nodes.translation: Alright. Is the lease contract enough?

nodes.speaker: landlord
nodes.translation: I understand. When do you need it?
nodes.wrongAnswerResponse: Prepáčte... to nie je odpoveď na moju otázku.
nodes.wrongAnswerTranslation: Sorry... that's not an answer to my question.

choices.text: Čím skôr. Stretnutie mám o dva týždne.
choices.translation: As soon as possible. My appointment is in two weeks.

choices.text: Do konca týždňa, ak sa dá.
choices.translation: By the end of the week, if possible.

nodes.speaker: landlord
nodes.text: Dobre. Poznám notára na Hlavnej ulici.
nodes.translation: Alright. I know a notary on Hlavná street.

nodes.speaker: landlord
nodes.translation: I will have it done by Friday.
nodes.wrongAnswerResponse: Prepáčte? Len sa pýtam kedy to potrebujete...
nodes.wrongAnswerTranslation: Excuse me? I'm just asking when you need it...

questions.question: What does 'potvrdenie o ubytovaní' mean?
questions.explanation: Potvrdenie = confirmation/proof. Ubytovanie = accommodation. This is the key document name to know at the Foreign Police.

questions.question: The landlord offered the lease contract. Is that enough?
choices: Yes – if it has your address
Only if it is less than 90 days old
questions.explanation: The Foreign Police require a notarised statement (notársky overené vyhlásenie). A regular lease contract may not be accepted.

questions.question: 'Mám to hotové do piatku.' What did the landlord promise?
choices: He will have it ready by Friday
He will go to the police on Friday
He will post it on Friday
questions.answer: He will have it ready by Friday
questions.explanation: Mám hotové = I have it done/ready. Do piatku = by Friday. A very common phrase in Slovak – you will hear it often.

questions.question: Why should you ask your landlord early – not the day before?
choices: Because the notary process takes time
Because the Foreign Police need time to check it
questions.answer: Because the notary process takes time
questions.explanation: Getting a notarised document means the landlord must book and visit a notary. This can take several days. Always ask at least 2 weeks before your appointment.

scenarios.situation: You need to book an appointment at the Foreign Police. Where do you do this?
choices: Call the Foreign Police by phone
Walk in on any working day
Ask your employer to book it
scenarios.answerMeaning: The official appointment booking system is at cudzinec.minv.sk. Walk-ins are rarely accepted — always book in advance. Waiting times in Bratislava can be 4â€“8 weeks.

scenarios.situation: The officer says: 'Dokument musí byť apostilovaný a úradne preložený.' What must you do?
choices: Get the document stamped at the Slovak embassy
Get the document apostilled AND translated by a certified translator
Submit the document in the original language
scenarios.answer: Get the document apostilled AND translated by a certified translator
scenarios.answerMeaning: Apostilovaný = apostilled. Úradne preložený = officially/certified translated. Both are required — either alone is not enough.

scenarios.situation: You are applying for temporary residence for employment. Your company's HR asks what document they need to provide. What is it?
choices: A letter saying you are a good employee
scenarios.answerMeaning: Pracovná zmluva = employment contract. This is the key 'purpose of stay' document for employment-based residence.

scenarios.situation: How many passport photos do you need to bring to the Foreign Police appointment?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Always bring 2 passport photos. Size: 3cm × 3.5cm, white background, biometric quality.

scenarios.situation: What is the minimum monthly income you must prove when applying for temporary residence?
scenarios.answerMeaning: The current minimum is €284.13 per month (subsistence minimum as of July 2025). Show a bank statement with at least this amount multiplied by the months of your planned stay. For stays over 1 year: minimum €3,409.56 total (12 × monthly minimum).

referenceCard.title: Temporary Residence — Reference Card
referenceCard.unlockedMessage: 🎉 You completed the Temporary Residence lesson! Your reference card is now saved to your profile.

checklist.warning: Bring original + photocopy of ALL pages
Must be APOSTILLED + certified Slovak translation. Cannot be older than 90 days.
Notarised landlord statement OR signed lease. Must cover 6+ months.
Bank statement showing min. €284.13/month. For stays over 1 year: min. €3,409.56 total (12 × monthly minimum).
Employment contract / business registration / enrollment letter — depends on your purpose
Verify current amount at minv.sk before your appointment

keyFacts: You can stay in Slovakia legally while waiting for the decision
Permit is for ONE purpose only — employment, study, business etc.
Apply for renewal before your permit expires — well in advance, since processing takes up to 90 days
All foreign documents must be apostilled AND translated by a certified Slovak translator
Documents cannot be older than 90 days when submitted
Minimum funds: €284.13/month (subsistence minimum, July 2025). For stays over 1 year: €3,409.56 total (12 × monthly minimum)

keyPhrase.english: I would like to apply for a temporary residence permit.
keyPhrase.slovak: Chcel/a by som požiadať o prechodný pobyt.

referenceCard.freeHelp: IOM Migration Information Centre: mic.iom.sk — free legal advice for foreigners
